LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION AND BECAUSE THE RULES THAT WOULD GOVERN THE ARBITRATION WERE NOT PROVIDED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE THE EXCEPTION FOR INJUNCTIVE AND SIMILAR EQUITABLE RELIEF OPERATES IN FAVOR OF EMPLOYERS AND GOES FAR BEYOND MERELY ACKNOWLEDGING THE STATUTORY AVAILABILITY OF INTERIM RELIEF. THE COURT DECLINES TO SEVER THE TERM BECAUSE IT IS INTEGRAL TO THE AGREEMENT THE PARTIES REACHED CONCERNING THE SCOPE OF WHAT WOULD BE ARBITRATED, AND ITS SEVERANCE WOULD IMPOSE ON THE PARTIES OBLIGATIONS TO WHICH THEY DID NOT AGREE. THIS IS TRUE EVEN THOUGH INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE BECAUSE AN EMPLOYER CANNOT IMPOSE UNCONSCIONABLE TERMS ONLY TO BACK AWAY ONCE CHALLENGED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS PETER HEELAN'S AND PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL IS CONTINUED FROM AUG-27-2013 TO SEP-17-2013 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE PRO TEM: JAMES P. WATSON; CLERK L. FONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER HEELAN AND PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND PRODUCE DOCUMENTS, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL IS DENIED. THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE JURISDICTION TO RULE ON THE MOTION TO COMPEL AND FOR SANCTIONS. CCP 916 PROVIDES THAT "THE PERFECTING OF AN APPEAL STAYS PROCEEDINGS IN THE TRIAL COURT'UPON THE MATTERS EMBRACED THEREIN OR AFFECTED THEREBY'" AN APPEAL OF AN ORDER DENYING ARBITRATION AFFECTS THE ENTIRE CASE. PRUDENTIAL-BACHE SECURITIES, INC. V. SUPERIOR COURT (1988) 201 CAL.APP.3D 924, 925. THUS, AN APPEAL FROM THE DENIAL OF A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AUTOMATICALLY STAYS ALL FURTHER TRIAL COURT PROCEEDINGS ON THE MERITS. VARIAN MEDICAL SYSTEMS, INC. V. DELFINO (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 180, 190. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, MTN TO COMPEL FURTHERE RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES-EMPLOYMENT LAW (SET ONE), AND REQ. FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET ONE), IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 1, THE MOTION IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHALL RESPOND TO PLAINTIFF'S PROPOSED LIMITED INTERROGATORY: "AS TO THE DOCUMENTS PRODUCED OR DESIGNATED AS PRIVILEGED IN RESPONSE TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, WHERE WERE THE DOCUMENTS KEPT IN THE NORMAL COURSE OF BUSINESS?" AS TO THE DRAFT MEMORANDUM, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE DOCUMENT IS PROTECTED BY ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE AND DEFENDANT HAS NOT WAIVED THAT PRIVILEGE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT STATING THAT DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE AMENDED RESPONSES WITHIN 15 DAYS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976; DEPT. 302. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PETER HEELAN'S JOINDER IN MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS PROCEDURALLY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE IT IS A CONTRACT OF ADHESION AND BECAUSE THE RULES THAT WOULD GOVERN THE ARBITRATION WERE NOT PROVIDED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE BECAUSE THE EXCEPTION FOR INJUNCTIVE AND SIMILAR EQUITABLE RELIEF OPERATES IN FAVOR OF EMPLOYERS AND GOES FAR BEYOND MERELY ACKNOWLEDGING THE STATUTORY AVAILABILITY OF INTERIM RELIEF. THE COURT DECLINES TO SEVER THE TERM BECAUSE IT IS INTEGRAL TO THE AGREEMENT THE PARTIES REACHED CONCERNING THE SCOPE OF WHAT WOULD BE ARBITRATED, AND ITS SEVERANCE WOULD IMPOSE ON THE PARTIES OBLIGATIONS TO WHICH THEY DID NOT AGREE. THIS IS TRUE EVEN THOUGH INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE BECAUSE AN EMPLOYER CANNOT IMPOSE UNCONSCIONABLE TERMS ONLY TO BACK AWAY ONCE CHALLENGED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Furthere Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Req. For Production Of Documents (Set One)
Off Calendar Jan-05-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BRENDAN M. BROWNFIELD of Burnham & Brown, Tel No. (510) 835-6732 appearing for Defendants Paul Ryan Associates, Inc. and Peter Heelan;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - Court and defense counsel discussed defense's request for more time to respond than what was stated in Plaintiff's proposed order. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF TERRENCE BARNUM'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law (Set One), And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) is granted in part and denied in part. As to Special Interrogatory No. 1, the motion is granted. Defendant shall respond to Plaintiff's proposed limited interrogatory: "As to the documents produced or designated as privileged in response to the request for production of documents, where were the documents kept in the normal course of business?" As to the Draft Memorandum, the motion is denied. The document is protected by attorney-client privilege and Defendant has not waived that privilege. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT STATING THAT DEFENDANT SHALL SERVE AMENDED RESPONSES WITHIN 15 DAYS. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses And Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew R. Shalauta, Esq. of Burnham Brown, Tel. 510.835.6708, for defendants and moving party;
William A. Bogdan, Esq. of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer, Tel. 415.397.2800, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETER HEELAN and PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses And Produce Documents, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel is denied. The Court does not have jurisdiction to rule on the motion to compel and for sanctions. CCP 916 provides that "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court'upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby'" An appeal of an order denying arbitration affects the entire case. Prudential-Bache Securities, Inc. v. Superior Court (1988) 201 Cal.App.3d 924, 925. Thus, an appeal from the denial of a motion to compel arbitration automatically stays all further trial court proceedings on the merits. Varian Medical Systems, Inc. v. Delfino (2005) 35 Cal.4th 180, 190. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings; Index Of Exhibits
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JULIE FIEBER, ESQ., 415.397.2800, FOR PLAINTIFF TERRENCE BARNUM.
ANDREW SHALAUTA, ESQ., 510.444.6800, FOR DEFENDANTS RYAN ASSOCIATES INC. AND PETER HEELAN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PAUL RYAN ASSOCIATES INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is Denied. The arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it is a contract of adhesion and because the rules that would govern the arbitration were not provided. The arbitration agreement is substantively unconscionable because the exception for injunctive and similar equitable relief operates in favor of employers and goes far beyond merely acknowledging the statutory availability of interim relief. The Court declines to sever the term because it is integral to the agreement the parties reached concerning the scope of what would be arbitrated, and its severance would impose on the parties obligations to which they did not agree. This is true even though injunctive relief is not at issue in this case because an employer cannot impose unconscionable terms only to back away once challenged. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Joinder Of Deft Peter Heelan Nrtc Of Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JULIE FIEBER, ESQ., 415.397.2800, FOR PLAINTIFF TERRENCE BARNUM.
ANDREW SHALAUTA, ESQ., 510.444.6800, FOR DEFENDANTS RYAN ASSOCIATES INC. AND PETER HEELAN.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT PETER HEELAN'S Joinder in Motion To Compel Arbitration is Denied. The arbitration agreement is procedurally unconscionable because it is a contract of adhesion and because the rules that would govern the arbitration were not provided. The arbitration agreement is substantively unconscionable because the exception for injunctive and similar equitable relief operates in favor of employers and goes far beyond merely acknowledging the statutory availability of interim relief. The Court declines to sever the term because it is integral to the agreement the parties reached concerning the scope of what would be arbitrated, and its severance would impose on the parties obligations to which they did not agree. This is true even though injunctive relief is not at issue in this case because an employer cannot impose unconscionable terms only to back away once challenged. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
